item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I also have regular bouts of sleep paralysis with accompanying nightmare-hallucinations, so this struck a very disturbing chord with me. You did a good job capturing the feverish almost-logic of it. I'd like to see more, but it's already worth a +1 as is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-2302-1: There's a big storm coming in against the wide purple sky. Covering the stars, Doctor Mommy. Covering the stars with sleepy rain and thunder. (There is a booming noise.) I'm scared, Doctor Mommy! I'm scared!

SCP-2302-1: You're wrong. Why crawl inside you when you aren't even real. Why, you could be just another you! Made of throw-up.

SCP-2302-1: I am from the tower at the top of the tall street with the houses and the trees and the road goes to the blue mountain by the deep canyon.

Teenage Horrors, amiright? +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Changed those two words as I agree they didn't quite fit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And this, I should note, was absolutely my initial take.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don’t really feel the loudspeaker and turning people into vomit ideas were really married well for me. I think part of it comes from the interview reading weird, with the mix of childlike sentiment and big words that felt out of place such as “fascinating” and “smokestack” making it not really sound like a kid. I don’t know if you were intending for that dichotomy to be there but it didn’t add anything to the article for me. I think with some additional development to make the concepts blend more smoothly this could be pretty cool. For now I’m downvoting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wasn't sure about this at first. The initial interview definitely sounds like a kid, maybe one who's delirious or otherwise compromised, but the second is kind of your standard "creepy voice over the intercom" shtick. That said, the potato chips and vomit motif presents a spooky internal logic to the whole proceeding, though I wasn't a fan of people turning into vomit randomly at the end. Like, there's just nothing you can do about that, though the containment procedures are about the best countermeasure, I guess.

That said, finding out this is based on a childhood nightmare makes the whole thing pop into focus, and now I'm thinking I should try writing something along those lines, too. :)

item.title: *squelch*
item.content:encoded:
This is the original "happy funtime game plan" + my childhood nightmares. For best results read this article with sleep paralysis.

Special thanks to Bogleech for introducing me to the original, and to Jacob Conwell and mlister for crit.
